title: Zanthoxylum parvifoliolum
taxonomy: Zanthoxylum parvifoliolum is classified within the family Rutaceae, which is commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This family is part of the order Sapindales, a group of flowering plants that includes other families such as Anacardiaceae and Meliaceae. The order Sapindales is further classified within the clade Rosids, which is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Zanthoxylum parvifoliolum is also classified within the genus Zanthoxylum, a group of flowering plants that are often found in warm and temperate regions. The classification of Zanthoxylum parvifoliolum reflects its evolutionary relationships with other plants within the Rutaceae family.
uses: Zanthoxylum parvifoliolum, commonly known as the small-leaved prickly ash, has been found to possess medicinal properties. The plant's bark and roots contain bioactive compounds that have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. In traditional medicine, Zanthoxylum parvifoliolum is used to alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory problems. Its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties also contribute to its potential use in wound healing and skin care. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential benefits of Zanthoxylum parvifoliolum.

intro: Zanthoxylum parvifoliolum, also known as Korean prickly ash, is a type of deciduous shrub or small tree native to East Asia, characterized by its small leaves and spiny branches. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, making it useful in treating various health issues such as pain, fever, and digestive problems. Bioactive constituents of Zanthoxylum parvifoliolum include lignans, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional Korean medicine, the plant is often made into herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including arthritis, respiratory issues, and skin problems.
